title: the-loop CLI configuration
description: Schema for the-loop's CLI config (cli-config.yaml: webhooks/polling/eventLog/collaborators/notifications), read by `the-loop gh-webhook`/`poll`/`sessions`/`events`. Independent of .the-loop/harness-config.schema.json (the per-repo HARNESS/plugin config): the CLI is expected to work across multiple repos, so its config resolves in priority order: --config/-c flag, $THE_LOOP_CLI_CONFIG env var, ./.the-loop/cli-config.yaml (repo-relative — an operator may choose to track it in a specific repo), else ~/.the-loop/cli-config.yaml. See decision-032. The operator's own notification recipients are DECLARED here (collaborators, same structure as collaborators.schema.json), never looked up from any repo's collaborators.yaml (issue-82, decision-035).

webhooks.description: Config for the the-loop CLI webhook receiver(s) (e.g. `the-loop gh-webhook start`).

secretEnv.description: Env var holding the GitHub webhook secret for HMAC verification (never stored in config).

events.description: GitHub event names of interest, e.g. pull_request_review_comment, workflow_run. Empty = accept all event types.

routing.description: Route received events to registered harness sessions via the session registry (docs/specs/issue-15/design.md, decision-016).

registryDir.description: Directory of per-session registry JSON files (git-ignored).

defaultHarness.description: Harness used when spawning a session for an unmatched event.

spawnOnUnmatched.description: Policy for events matching no registered session: 'never' log-and-drop; 'always' spawn+register a new session; 'labeled' spawn only when the issue/PR carries autoExecuteLabel.

autoExecuteLabel.description: Issue/PR label that opts a work item into autonomous execution (spawnOnUnmatched: labeled). Read from the webhook payload, no API call.

authorizedUsers.description: SECURITY (prompt-injection guard): GitHub logins whose actions the-loop may act on. Comments/reviews/labels/items authored by anyone not listed are ignored by BOTH the webhook receiver and the poller. REQUIRED to actually route anything: no fallback to any repo's harness (plugin) config (.the-loop/harness-config.yaml) — which logins may trigger the daemon is a CLI-config-only concern (issue-63 review). Empty means human-authored events are all ignored (fail closed). Each operator runs their own instance for their own logins.

spawnWorkdir.description: Working directory for sessions spawned on unmatched events. Used only when routing.workspace.root is unset; when a workspace root is configured, spawned sessions run in a per-work-item git worktree instead (issue-76, decision-034).

workspace.description: Per-work-item checkout workspace for spawned sessions (issue-76, decision-034). When `root` is set, the dispatcher checks out each event's repo under the workspace root (layout depends on `strategy`) and runs the spawned session there, instead of the static spawnWorkdir. Auth is the operator's own git credentials (e.g. `gh auth setup-git`).

root.description: Absolute path to the workspace root where repos are checked out (e.g. ~/.the-loop/workspace). Empty (the default) disables the workspace: spawned sessions fall back to spawnWorkdir.

strategy.description: Checkout layout. 'worktree': one shared clone per repo at <root>/<host>/<owner>/<repo> plus one git worktree per work item under <root>/.worktrees/… — concurrent work items on a repo share objects (cheap). 'clone': one folder per work item at <root>/.work-items/<slug>/ with a full clone of each repo it touches — self-contained and simpler to reason about/clean up when a work item spans multiple repos, at the cost of a full clone per work item.

cloneProtocol.description: Preferred clone URL scheme. 'https' uses the payload's clone_url (or https://<host>/<owner>/<repo>.git); 'ssh' uses ssh_url (or git@<host>:<owner>/<repo>.git).

defaultHost.description: Host directory used when the event payload carries no html_url to infer it from (e.g. the poller's leaner payloads). Set to your enterprise domain for a GHE deployment.

keepCheckoutOnClose.description: Keep a work item's checkout after its PR is merged/closed (for post-mortem) instead of removing it — the worktree (worktree strategy) or the work-item folder (clone strategy). In worktree strategy the shared per-repo clone is always kept regardless.

gitBinary.description: Path/name of the git binary used for clone/worktree operations.

runner.description: How spawned sessions are hosted (receiver-global): 'process' = headless one-shot subprocess; 'tmux' = interactive harness TUI in a named tmux session (loop-<slug>) humans can attach to (issue-32, decision-021).

webTerminal.description: Optional ttyd-served browser terminal onto the tmux sessions (runner: tmux). No auth of its own — access control is environmental: localhost, VPN, or the hosting provider's network (decision-021).

enabled.description: Serve tmux sessions over HTTP via ttyd (verified at receiver start).

host.description: Interface/IP ttyd binds. Keep 127.0.0.1 unless the network layer protects wider exposure.

maxConcurrentDispatches.description: Cap on harness dispatches running in parallel (per-session dispatch is always serialized).

dedupCacheSize.description: Bounded LRU of X-GitHub-Delivery ids for at-most-once processing.

dispatchTimeoutSeconds.description: Timeout for a single harness resume/spawn subprocess.

promptTemplate.description: string.Template rendered into the prompt that resumes a session. the-loop's internal template; the dispatcher falls back to a built-in default when the path is absent (the case in a project repo, which does not carry the-loop's templates). Set a repo-relative path to override.

spawnPromptTemplate.description: string.Template rendered into the prompt for a newly spawned (auto-execute) session — kicks off the-loop's work-on flow. the-loop's internal template; the dispatcher falls back to a built-in default when the path is absent. Set a repo-relative path to override.

harnessArgs.description: Extra CLI args per harness (e.g. permission flags). The dispatcher never widens permissions itself.

reactions.description: Dispatch-lifecycle emoji reactions on the triggering GitHub entity (issue-84): when the dispatcher picks an event up it reacts with `started` on the comment that triggered it (or the issue/PR itself for presence/label/review events), then `completed` or `error` from the dispatch outcome. Shared by the webhook receiver AND the poller (like the rest of `routing`). Best-effort via the operator's own `gh` CLI: a reaction failure never affects the dispatch, and a missing `gh`, a non-GitHub provider, or an event with no reactable target is a silent no-op. GitHub's reaction palette is fixed (+1 -1 laugh confused heart hooray rocket eyes) — ✅/⁉️ do not exist, so the defaults map each state to the closest supported emoji.

enabled.description: Opt-in (default false): reacting is the daemon's first WRITE to GitHub and posts with the operator's own gh credentials, so it is never turned on silently.

started.description: Reaction added when the event is dequeued for delivery/spawn (👀 by default). Empty string skips this state.

completed.description: Reaction added when the dispatch succeeds (🎉 by default — GitHub has no ✅ reaction). Empty string skips this state.

error.description: Reaction added when the dispatch fails or the worker crashes (😕 by default — GitHub has no ⁉️ reaction). Empty string skips this state.

ghBinary.description: Path/name of the gh CLI used to post reactions (uses the operator's existing gh auth, like the poller's reads).

polling.description: Config for the the-loop CLI poller (`the-loop poll start`) — a pull-based, provider-agnostic alternative to the webhook receiver for hosts a webhook cannot reach. Each entry in `sources` selects a provider (e.g. github) the system interfaces with; the core carries no provider-specific knobs. Dispatch behaviour (harness, runner, spawn policy, templates, registry) is reused from `webhooks.ghWebhook.routing`.

intervalSeconds.description: Seconds between poll cycles (applies to all sources).

stateFile.description: Durable JSON tracking which comments each item has processed (cross-poll/restart dedup). Git-ignored runtime state.

maxRetries.description: Per-event delivery attempts before the poller gives up (issue-80). A spawn or comment forward whose dispatch keeps failing is retried each cycle up to this many attempts; after that the poller logs a terminal failure (poll.spawn_failed / poll.comment_failed) and ignores the event on later polls until new activity re-arms it. An in-flight (still-processing) dispatch is not counted as a failed attempt.

sources.description: Ordered list of provider-specific poll sources. Each entry MUST name a `provider`; the remaining keys are that provider's own config (unknown to the core).

provider.description: Which poll provider handles this source. Only `github` ships today (jira is reserved for a follow-up).

label.description: Label gating what this source polls. Empty = reuse webhooks.ghWebhook.routing.autoExecuteLabel.

repos.description: [github] Repositories to poll as OWNER/REPO. REQUIRED: no fallback to any repo's harness (plugin) config (.the-loop/harness-config.yaml) — which repos to watch is a CLI-config-only concern (issue-63 review). A source with no repos discovers nothing (list_work_items raises).

ghBinary.description: [github] Path/name of the gh CLI (uses the user's existing gh auth).

collaborators.description: The OPERATOR'S own notification recipients — the same collaborator structure as .the-loop/collaborators.schema.json ($defs/collaborator), declared here rather than looked up: the CLI daemon never reads any repo's collaborators.yaml (issue-82, decision-035). Typically just the operator themself; roles are used by notifications.events to pick recipients.

notifications.description: Which DAEMON-side events notify which roles from the operator's `collaborators` above (issue-82, decision-035). Disjoint from the harness-side taxonomy in harness-config.yaml (decision pending, PR review, …) — those are raised by the harness inside a repo checkout; these concern the daemon itself.

events.description: Event → roles to notify. Omitting an event means nobody is notified for it.

work-item-spawned.description: A session was spawned for a work item (auto-execute label or spawnOnUnmatched: always).

dispatch-failed.description: A harness dispatch failed terminally (poll.spawn_failed / poll.comment_failed after maxRetries, or a webhook dispatch error).

session-died.description: A registered tmux session was found dead (and respawned when possible — issue-80).

event-dropped-unauthorized.description: An event was dropped by the authorized-actor guard (routing.authorizedUsers).

eventLog.description: Structured JSONL event log of every routing/dispatch/session decision made by the-loop's CLI processes (gh-webhook, poll, sessions). Queried with `the-loop events`; schema in skills/the-loop/reference/observability.md (decision-025). Top-level here (not nested under an `observability` key) — the CLI config has no dev/runtime log-level concept, that stays with the HARNESS config's `observability.devLevel`/`runtimeLevel`/`browserLogging`.
